response.justification: Candidate has 13+ years of software development experience with strong full-stack and systems-programming credentials (DLang, Node.js, TypeScript), but the role specifically requires a hands-on C++ developer with deep STL, multi-threading, MQTT, and IEC 62443 industrial-cybersecurity expertise. C++ exposure is limited to a ~6-month stint at National Instruments in 2014-2015 and an undergraduate thesis. There is no evidence of recent C++ work, industrial automation experience, message-broker architectures, or secure development under IEC 62443. The profile is a poor technical match despite seniority.
strengths: 13+ years of senior engineering experience across web, mobile, desktop, and embedded
Strong software craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, pairing, trunk-based development
Some past C/C++ work at National Instruments and low-latency systems experience in DLang
concerns: C++/STL not listed in current skills; last C++ role was a decade ago
No experience with IEC 62443, SDL, MQTT, or industrial automation protocols (Modbus, OPC UA, DNP 3.0)
Career trajectory is web/full-stack and AI tooling, not high-performance C++ platform engineering

key_factors.factor: No industrial cybersecurity (IEC 62443) or SDL background
Strong senior-level engineering breadth and craftsmanship
